site.info.signature: dsgo.site.info(): Promise<{ title: string; description: string; url: string; admin_email: string; language: string; timezone: string; gmt_offset: number; date_format: string; time_format: string }>
site.info.description: Returns site identity + locale/date settings. One call gives the full SiteInfo shape; no paging, no parameters.
examples: const info = await dsgo.site.info(); document.title = info.title;

posts.list.signature: dsgo.posts.list(query?: PostsQuery): Promise<{ items: Post[]; total: number; total_pages: number }>
posts.list.description: Paginated list of posts. PostsQuery accepts: type? (CPT slug, default 'post'), per_page?, page?, search?, category?, tag?, orderby? ('date'|'modified'|'title'|'id'), order? ('asc'|'desc'), status?. Returns items + pagination totals (read X-WP-Total / X-WP-TotalPages on the wire). The current admin's caps apply: published posts always; drafts only if editable.
examples: const { items, total } = await dsgo.posts.list({ per_page: 20 });
const recipes = await dsgo.posts.list({ type: 'recipe', per_page: 10 });

posts.get.signature: dsgo.posts.get(id: number, opts?: { type?: string }): Promise<Post>
posts.get.description: Fetch a single post by ID. Pass opts.type for a CPT lookup (defaults to 'post'). Returns the full Post object including content, excerpt, dates, author, and content_styles (when the manifest opts in).

pages.list.signature: dsgo.pages.list(query?: PostsQuery): Promise<{ items: Post[]; total: number; total_pages: number }>
pages.list.description: Paginated list of pages. Same query shape as posts.list (minus category/tag, which pages don't have).

pages.get.description: Fetch a single page by ID. Same Post shape as posts.get.

user.current.signature: dsgo.user.current(): Promise<{ id: number; name: string; slug: string; email: string; avatar_url: string; roles: string[] } | null>
user.current.description: Returns the currently-logged-in user, or null when the visitor is logged out (NOT a rejection — null is the legitimate anonymous case). Inline-mode apps rendered to logged-out visitors should expect null.

user.can.description: Capability check for the current user. Returns false (not a rejection) when no session is present. Useful for showing admin-only UI conditionally without a role check.

storage.app.get.description: Per-app storage shared by all users — use for app-wide settings. No manifest permission required. Values are JSON-serialized; the bridge rejects non-serializable values.

storage.app.set.description: Set per-app storage. Total quota: 64KB per app across all keys.

storage.user.get.description: Per-user, per-app storage. Each user has their own values; use for in-app preferences. Returns null when no session is present.

storage.user.set.description: Set per-user storage. Quota: 16KB per (user, app) pair. Requires a logged-in session.

bridge.ping.signature: dsgo.bridge.ping(): Promise<{ ok: true; bridge_version: number; server_time: string }>
bridge.ping.description: Liveness check. Confirms the bridge is reachable; returns the protocol version and the server's current ISO 8601 timestamp. No permission required.

abilities.list.description: List the WordPress Abilities matching the manifest's abilities.consumes allowlist. Each AbilityDescriptor has name, label, description, category, input_schema, output_schema, and annotations (readonly/destructive/idempotent). Requires permissions.read: ['abilities'].

abilities.invoke.signature: dsgo.abilities.invoke<T = unknown>(name: string, args?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T>
abilities.invoke.description: Invoke a registered ability. The name MUST be in abilities.consumes. Args are JSON-passed to the ability's PHP execute_callback. Result shape depends on the ability — declare a type parameter to type-check the response.

ai.prompt.signature: dsgo.ai.prompt(params: { messages: Array<{role: 'user'|'assistant'|'system', content: string}>; max_tokens?: number; tools?: 'auto'|string[] }): Promise<AiPromptResult>
ai.prompt.description: Single-shot AI completion via the site's WP 7.0 Connector. Synchronous (no streaming). When tools is set, the model can call abilities the app declares in abilities.consumes — result.tool_calls echoes each invocation with its result.
examples: const r = await dsgo.ai.prompt({ messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Summarize: ' + text }], max_tokens: 200 });
const r2 = await dsgo.ai.prompt({ messages: [...], tools: 'auto' });

email.send.signature: dsgo.email.send(params: { to: 'admin'|'current_user'; subject: string; body: string; isHtml?: boolean; replyTo?: string }): Promise<{ sent: true }>
email.send.description: Send transactional email through wp_mail(). The current stub only accepts symbolic recipients ('admin' → site admin email, 'current_user' → logged-in user's email) — arbitrary addresses ship with 2026-05-09-email-design.md. body is plain by default; pass isHtml: true for HTML (sanitized via wp_kses_post). Per-app daily cap enforced server-side.
examples: await dsgo.email.send({ to: 'admin', subject: 'Order #42 confirmed', body: 'Thanks!' });

media.upload.signature: dsgo.media.upload(file: Blob, opts?: { filename?: string; altText?: string }): Promise<MediaUploadResult>
media.upload.description: Upload a Blob (or File) to the WP media library. Multipart wire format. Returns { id, url, mime_type, filename, width|null, height|null, alt_text }. Requires the current user to have 'upload_files'. Core opt-out: every app gets this method unless manifest declares media.uploads: false.
examples: const result = await dsgo.media.upload(input.files[0], { altText: 'Hero image' });

router.navigate.description: Navigate to a different in-bundle route. Inline-mode apps only — no-op in iframe mode. The parent enforces the mount-prefix and rejects paths in WordPress-reserved prefixes (/wp-admin, /wp-json, etc.).

commerce.products.list.signature: dsgo.commerce.products.list(query?: CommerceProductsQuery): Promise<{ items: CommerceProduct[]; total: number; total_pages: number }>
commerce.products.list.description: List products from the active commerce provider (WooCommerce in v1). Tries the woocommerce/list-products ability first, falls back to REST. Requires permissions.read: ['commerce'].

commerce.products.get.description: Fetch one product by ID. Tries woocommerce/get-product ability first, falls back to REST.

commerce.cart.get.description: Current shopper's cart: items, totals, applied coupons, shipping requirement.

commerce.cart.add_item.signature: dsgo.commerce.cart.addItem(params: { id: number; quantity?: number; variation?: { attribute: string; value: string }[] }): Promise<CommerceCart>
commerce.cart.add_item.description: Add a product to the cart. Returns the updated cart. Note: the bridge wire method is named commerce.cart.add_item but the client API uses addItem.

commerce.cart.update_item.signature: dsgo.commerce.cart.updateItem(params: { key: string; quantity: number }): Promise<CommerceCart>
commerce.cart.update_item.description: Update quantity on an existing line. Quantity 0 removes the line.

commerce.cart.remove_item.description: Remove a cart line by its server-issued key.

commerce.checkout.open_hosted_page.signature: dsgo.commerce.checkout.openHostedPage(params?: { return_to?: string }): Promise<{ url: string; navigated: boolean }>
commerce.checkout.open_hosted_page.description: Hand off to WooCommerce's hosted checkout. The bridge navigates the top window directly (iframe mode: parent navigates; inline mode: current window). WC's session cookie carries the cart through the navigation.
examples: // navigation already dispatched — r.url is informational

help.method.signature: dsgo.help.method(name: string): Promise<{ signature: string; description: string; errors: string[]; examples: string[] }>
help.method.description: Look up any bridge method's full documentation at runtime. Always available — no manifest permission required. The harness uses this to discover methods without enumerating every signature in the system prompt. Returns 'not_found' for unknown methods.

http.fetch.signature: dsgo.http.fetch(url: string, init?: { method?: 'GET'|'POST'|'PUT'|'PATCH'|'DELETE'|'HEAD'; headers?: Record<string,string>; body?: string; timeout_ms?: number }): Promise<{ ok: true; status: number; headers: Record<string,string>; body: unknown }>
http.fetch.description: Server-mediated outbound HTTP — the app never holds credentials. URL must be https; host must match the manifest's permissions.http allowlist (exact or *.subdomain wildcard). Header values may include {{ALIAS}} tokens that get substituted from the per-app sodium-encrypted secret vault before the request is issued. The proxy strips Cookie / Set-Cookie / Host both inbound and outbound, blocks SSRF (private/loopback/link-local IPs + self-target), pins DNS to the validated IP on the cURL transport to defeat rebinding, and never follows 30x redirects. JSON content-type responses auto-parse into a structured body; everything else stays a raw string.
examples: const r = await dsgo.http.fetch('https://api.stripe.com/v1/charges', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Authorization': 'Bearer {{STRIPE_SECRET}}', 'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded' }, body: 'amount=100&currency=usd' });
